God warned Solomon, now that he had newly built and dedicated the temple, so that he and his people might not become proud but fear. After all the services we can perform, we stand upon the same terms with the Lord as before. Nothing can purchase for us liberty to sin, nor would a true believer desire such freedom. He would rather be disciplined by the Lord than be allowed to continue easily and prosperously in sin.

Solomon gave Hiram twenty cities. Hiram did not like them. If Solomon would please him, let it be in his own area of expertise by becoming his partner in trade, as he did. See how the providence of God suits this earth to the various temperaments of people, and the dispositions of people to the earth, and all for the good of mankind in general.

Here is a further account of Solomon's greatness. He began at the right end, for he built God’s house first, and finished that before he began his own; then God blessed him, and he prospered in all his other buildings. Let piety begin, and profit follow; leave pleasure to the last. Whoever takes pains for the glory of God, and to profit others, is likely to have an advantage. Canaan, the holy land, the glory of all lands, had no gold in it; which shows that the best produce is that which supports life, our own and others’; such things did Canaan produce. Solomon gained much by his merchandise, and yet has directed us to a better trade, within reach of the poorest. Wisdom is better than the merchandise of silver, and its gain is better than fine gold.
